content:
Stories (continued) — HRREC-82977

StoryGap score (%)PM lensQA lensXO lensVerdictSuggested missing BDD (Given/When/Then)

HRREC-92007
PH: Recruiter can switch between tabs for candidate and agency comms

45% — PM isolation story vs QA URL tamper vs XO participant scoping still need shared security sign-off.

  • Jira — PH: Recruiter can switch between tabs for candidate and agency comms: confirm acceptance scenarios still match recruiter language after recent edits.
  • Salomon — Agency / UDMF: duplicate agency submissions depend on BP CRF vs UDMF-aware rule; isolation tests need explicit internal vs agency accounts.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92007 (PH: Recruiter can switch between tabs for candidate…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • Functional knowledge — Consent and audit expectations for candidate-visible email still apply—tie any new surface to comms policy review.
  • Jira — Exercise negatives implied by PH: Recruiter can switch between tabs for candidate and agency comms: record tenant SKU (SSP growth) and mail lane used.
  • Salomon — Email Analytics may reject empty subject lines—pair blank-body template issues with vendor status rows when reproducing.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92007 (PH: Recruiter can switch between tabs for candidate…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • XO MCP — row focus HRREC-92007: PH: Recruiter can switch between tabs for candidate and agency comms — map SUV bindings for this slice; Tier B catalog search only (no per-hook proof in this pass).
  • XO MCP — searched cl: Conversation (and related conversational UI classes) as anchor for thread container work.
  • XO MCP — risk: vendor mail rejections may surface as generic errors—pair UI copy with vendor status rows.
  • Finding: HRREC-92007 — Edge cases (slow network, double submit) are implied more than written—false-positive bug risk until spelled out.
  • Recommended next step: Run one cross-browser smoke on the primary path after each merge train batch for this epic.

Scenario: REST parity spot-check (key-specific)
Given tenant staged for HRREC-92007 with logged mail mode
When the recruiter performs the behaviour described in: Recruiter can switch between tabs for candidate and agency comms
Then UI, persisted thread state, and captured network payloads align for this slice

HRREC-92008
PH: Agency cannot see recruiter conversation

45% — PM isolation story vs QA URL tamper vs XO participant scoping still need shared security sign-off.

  • Jira — PH: Agency cannot see recruiter conversation: confirm acceptance scenarios still match recruiter language after recent edits.
  • Salomon — Agency / UDMF: duplicate agency submissions depend on BP CRF vs UDMF-aware rule; isolation tests need explicit internal vs agency accounts.

  • Functional knowledge — Consent and audit expectations for candidate-visible email still apply—tie any new surface to comms policy review.
  • Jira — Exercise negatives implied by PH: Agency cannot see recruiter conversation: record tenant SKU (SSP growth) and mail lane used.
  • Salomon — Email Analytics may reject empty subject lines—pair blank-body template issues with vendor status rows when reproducing.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92008 (PH: Agency cannot see recruiter conversation): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • XO MCP — row focus HRREC-92008: PH: Agency cannot see recruiter conversation — map SUV bindings for this slice; Tier B catalog search only (no per-hook proof in this pass).
  • XO MCP — searched cl: Conversation Message (+ Derived, Status, Sender) for delivery tags, read/unread, purge visibility.
  • XO MCP — risk: My Conversations refactor dependency called out in Jira—needs OE alignment before QA signs routing.
  • Finding: HRREC-92008 — Edge cases (slow network, double submit) are implied more than written—false-positive bug risk until spelled out.
  • Recommended next step: Attach the exact mail mode and template name used when the behaviour reproduces (or fails).

HRREC-92009
PH: Recruiter can see both closed and non-closed conversation in the same panel if one conversation is still active

38% — PM slice value clear; QA tenant limits; XO catalog present but per-hook proof still SUV work.

  • Jira — PH: Recruiter can see both closed and non-closed conversation in the same panel if one conversation is still active: confirm acceptance scenarios still match recruiter language after recent edits.
  • Salomon — Agency / UDMF: duplicate agency submissions depend on BP CRF vs UDMF-aware rule; isolation tests need explicit internal vs agency accounts.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92009 (PH: Recruiter can see both closed and non-closed co…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • Functional knowledge — Consent and audit expectations for candidate-visible email still apply—tie any new surface to comms policy review.
  • Jira — Exercise negatives implied by PH: Recruiter can see both closed and non-closed conversation in the …: record tenant SKU (SSP growth) and mail lane used.
  • Salomon — Email Analytics may reject empty subject lines—pair blank-body template issues with vendor status rows when reproducing.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92009 (PH: Recruiter can see both closed and non-closed co…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • XO MCP — row focus HRREC-92009: PH: Recruiter can see both closed and non-closed conversation in the same panel if one … — map SUV bindings for this slice; Tier B catalog search only (no per-hook proof in this pass).
  • XO MCP — searched Recruiting Email PRU/PU paths (Candidate Email create, External/Internal candidate parms) for send/receive wiring.
  • XO MCP — risk: My Conversations refactor dependency called out in Jira—needs OE alignment before QA signs routing.
  • Finding: HRREC-92009 — Tenant mail setup and template health still dominate false defects next to UI polish for this slice.
  • Recommended next step: Document tenant limits (attachment size, recipient count) beside UI acceptance text.

HRREC-92010
PH: Recruiter sees tags on messages showing updating state (sent not delivered)

38% — PM slice value clear; QA tenant limits; XO catalog present but per-hook proof still SUV work.

  • Jira — PH: Recruiter sees tags on messages showing updating state (sent not delivered): confirm acceptance scenarios still match recruiter language after recent edits.
  • Salomon — Recruiting Communications / tenant templates: blank-body risk when subject or body missing or template overrides recruiter draft (internal precedent on grid sends).
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92010 (PH: Recruiter sees tags on messages showing updatin…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • Functional knowledge — Consent and audit expectations for candidate-visible email still apply—tie any new surface to comms policy review.
  • Jira — Exercise negatives implied by PH: Recruiter sees tags on messages showing updating state (sent not …: record tenant SKU (SSP growth) and mail lane used.
  • Salomon — Email Analytics may reject empty subject lines—pair blank-body template issues with vendor status rows when reproducing.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92010 (PH: Recruiter sees tags on messages showing updatin…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • XO MCP — row focus HRREC-92010: PH: Recruiter sees tags on messages showing updating state (sent not delivered) — map SUV bindings for this slice; Tier B catalog search only (no per-hook proof in this pass).
  • XO MCP — searched cl: Conversation (and related conversational UI classes) as anchor for thread container work.
  • XO MCP — risk: duplicate REST paths for similar compose actions—assert the exact endpoint used by this UI path.
  • Finding: HRREC-92010 — Cross-story overlap on compose/send paths—risk of conflicting mocks unless QE shares one baseline tenant recipe.
  • Recommended next step: Log Default SMTP vs override and Recruiting Communications template state in each defect.

HRREC-92011
PH: Privacy Admin purges PDTs for 2-way email - Candidate Job App

48% — PM privacy scope vs QA recruiter-empty-state vs XO message retention need one matrix.

  • Jira — PH: Privacy Admin purges PDTs for 2-way email - Candidate Job App: confirm acceptance scenarios still match recruiter language after recent edits.
  • Salomon — Purge: Recruiting Communications PDT removes conversation messages, participants, recruiting email attachments, notification events—align recruiter-visible UI after purge with privacy comms.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92011 (PH: Privacy Admin purges PDTs for 2-way email - Can…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • Functional knowledge — Consent and audit expectations for candidate-visible email still apply—tie any new surface to comms policy review.
  • Jira — Exercise negatives implied by PH: Privacy Admin purges PDTs for 2-way email - Candidate Job App: record tenant SKU (SSP growth) and mail lane used.
  • Salomon — Email Analytics may reject empty subject lines—pair blank-body template issues with vendor status rows when reproducing.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-92011 (PH: Privacy Admin purges PDTs for 2-way email - Can…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • XO MCP — row focus HRREC-92011: PH: Privacy Admin purges PDTs for 2-way email - Candidate Job App — map SUV bindings for this slice; Tier B catalog search only (no per-hook proof in this pass).
  • XO MCP — searched cl: Conversation Message (+ Derived, Status, Sender) for delivery tags, read/unread, purge visibility.
  • XO MCP — risk: purge removes Conversation Message rows—confirm UI empty states vs archived placeholders after privacy job.
  • Finding: Privacy purge scope is broad; recruiter-visible thread state after purge needs explicit expectation.
  • Recommended next step: Pair privacy admin dry-run with recruiting UX on empty thread copy.

Scenario: REST parity spot-check (key-specific)
Given tenant staged for HRREC-92011 with logged mail mode
When the recruiter performs the behaviour described in: Privacy Admin purges PDTs for 2-way email - Candidate Job App
Then UI, persisted thread state, and captured network payloads align for this slice
